title: Fresh Light Mediterranean Egg Flatbread
description: A crisp, grain‑free flatbread topped with poached eggs, sun‑dried tomatoes, olives, and a zesty lemon herb drizzle—perfect for a light brunch or lunch.

ingredients.name: Paleo flatbread mixture
ingredients.amount: 2 cups almond flour, 1/4 cup coconut flour, 1 tsp baking powder, ½ tsp salt, 1 egg, 1/4 cup unsweetened almond milk, 2 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp dried oregano

ingredients.name: Poaching liquid
ingredients.amount: 1 cup water + 1 tsp white wine vinegar

instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). In a bowl, whisk together almond flour, coconut flour, baking powder, salt, egg, almond milk, olive oil, and oregano until smooth. Let rest 5 minutes.
Divide dough into 4 equal portions, roll each into an 8×8‑inch circle, and place on parchment‑lined baking sheet. Bake 12–15 minutes until golden and lightly crisp.

While the flatbread bakes, bring the poaching liquid to a gentle simmer over medium heat. Gently slide each egg into the water and poach for 3–4 minutes until the whites are set but yolks remain runny. Remove with a slotted spoon and set aside.
When the flatbread is done, let it cool for a minute, then drizzle 1 tablespoon olive oil over the top. Sprinkle sun‑dried tomatoes, chopped olives, and parsley evenly.
Squeeze lemon juice over the toppings, grate lemon zest, and lightly sprinkle salt and pepper.
Cut each flatbread into 4 wedges. Place one poached egg on top of each wedge.
Serve immediately, optionally with a side of fresh fruit or a light salad.
